claim: The next Bruges Triennial does not run in 2026; the fifth edition is scheduled 24 April–5 September 2027 under the theme 'After Birth – Structures of Togetherness', curated by Ulrika Flink and Helen Rix Runting.
quote: The fifth edition of Bruges Triennial will run from 24 April to 5 September 2027… entirely dedicated to After Birth – Structures of Togetherness.

claim: De Witte Lelie is an 11-room small-luxury Antwerp hotel set in three 16th-century white townhouses, member of Small Luxury Hotels of the World and awarded a Michelin Key.
quote: Hotel De Witte Lelie is an exquisite small luxury hotel located right in the centre of Antwerp… 11 rooms across three 16th-century white townhouses.

claim: Botanic Sanctuary Antwerp occupies a 15th-century monastery complex and is the city's only Leading Hotels of the World property, with a destination spa.
quote: Housed within a magnificent 15th-century monastery complex, Botanic Sanctuary Antwerp is the city's first—and only—member of The Leading Hotels of the World.

claim: The Hanswijkprocessie takes place in Mechelen on Sunday 10 May 2026, marking the 150th anniversary of the crowning of the Maria of Hanswijk statue with the theme 'MARIA, een kroon'.
quote: Op zondag 10 mei 2026 neemt de Hanswijkprocessie je mee op een unieke tocht door de stad Mechelen… 150-jarige kroningsjubileum.

claim: Namur and Dinant pair naturally for one weekend: trains between them run every 20–40 minutes and take about 30 minutes, with weekend half-price tickets reducing fares to about €4.25.
quote: Trains from Namur/Jambes to Dinant leave every 20 to 40 minutes or so and take about 30 minutes… At weekends, half price rail tickets are on offer reducing fares to only €4.25.

claim: There is no direct Ghent–Dinant train; the journey via Brussels-Midi takes about 3h 25m, making Dinant a stay-the-night destination rather than a day trip from Ghent.
quote: There is no direct train from Ghent to Dinant, however there are services departing from Gand-Saint-Pierre and arriving at Dinant via Bruxelles-Midi… approximately 3h 25m.

claim: Liège's Sunday La Batte market runs every Sunday 8:00–14:30 along the Meuse and is often called the largest and oldest public market in Belgium.
quote: The La Batte market on Sunday is an institution… often said to be the biggest and oldest public market in Belgium. It operates every Sunday from 8:00 to 14:30.

claim: La Boverie museum sits in Parc de la Boverie in the former Palais des Beaux-Arts (built for Liège's 1905 International Exposition) and holds 700+ French and Belgian paintings from the 1850s including Gauguin and Emile Claus.
quote: La Boverie is a renowned art museum located in the lush Parc de la Boverie, housed in the former Palais des beaux-arts de Liege… collection of over 700 French and Belgian paintings dating from the 1850s onwards.

claim: Leuven markets itself as Belgium's 'capital of love' for couples, recommending the University Library tower at Ladeuzeplein, the Great Beguinage cobblestones and the Dijlepark arch bridge as headline romantic stops.
quote: Leuven is the real capital of love… start high above the city at the top of the tower of the University Library in Ladeuzeplein… cobbled streets of the Great Beguinage or across the arch bridge in Dijlepark.

claim: Relais Bourgondisch Cruyce is a 16-room canalside boutique in Bruges at the meeting point of two historic canals, five minutes from Grote Markt, with antiques and works inspired by Botero, Chagall, Klimt and Matisse.
quote: Set within a centuries-old landmark at the meeting point of two historic canals… 16 individually designed rooms… curated collection of antiques and artworks inspired by masters such as Botero, Chagall, Klimt, and Matisse.

claim: Hotel de Tuilerieen sits directly on the Dijver canal in central Bruges and houses Restaurant Mémoire, a refined dining option for couples.
quote: Hotel de Tuilerieen… perfectly located on the canal, just minutes from the market place… refined dining at Restaurant Mémoire, backdropped by the Dijver canal.

claim: Made in Louise is a small Brussels boutique hotel near Avenue Louise, well-positioned for the Horta/Art Nouveau triangle of Saint-Gilles and Ixelles.
quote: Made in Louise - Brussels | Official Website | Best price guarantee -15%

claim: The Horta Museum at 23–25 rue Américaine in Saint-Gilles occupies Victor Horta's former town house and studio (1898–1901) and is one of four Horta houses inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list since 2000.
quote: The Horta Museum… is housed in Horta's former town house and workshop… built between 1898 and 1901 in Art Nouveau style… added to the UNESCO World Heritage list in 2000.

claim: Jazz Middelheim 2026 runs 23–25 May 2026 in Park Den Brandt over the Pentecost weekend, with a lineup including Cécile McLorin Salvant and the Buena Vista Orchestra.
quote: Jazz Middelheim 2026 takes place from 23 to 25 May 2026… in Park Den Brandt… Cécile McLorin Salvant… Buena Vista Orchestra.

claim: Dinant's headline experiences are the cliff-top Citadel reached by cable car (€14), the free Maison Sax birthplace museum on Rue Adolphe Sax 37 and Meuse river cruises (45-min, 2-hr to Freyr Castle, or 3-hr).
quote: You can find Maison Sax on Rue Adolphe Sax 37 and the museum is open daily from 9 am to 7 pm and is free to visit… cable car tickets costing €14… 45-minute boat tour to Anseremme and back… 2-hour boat tour… to Freyr.

claim: Mechelen reads as Belgium's underrated 'perfect-sized Burgundian city break' — Gothic St. Rumbold's tower with skywalk, Dijle canal walks and the De Vleeshalle food hall in an 1881 meat market — only ~20 minutes by train from Brussels or Antwerp.
quote: Mechelen, Belgium's Perfect-Sized Burgundian City Break… stunning Gothic architecture, picturesque canals, and a vibrant food scene without the tourist crowds… about 20 minutes by train from either Brussels or Antwerp.

claim: Ne5t Hotel & Spa in Namur is an adults-only 4* boutique in a renovated 17th-century farmhouse with 6 exclusive suites/duplexes, spa and large pool, marketed explicitly for couples.
quote: The Ne5t is the perfect place for a romantic getaway in Namur and the hotel is only accessible to adults… 17th-century farmhouse renovation into a paradise for couples, complete with spa, large swimming pool and 6 exclusive suites.

claim: Ghent–Brussels intercity trains run roughly every 30 minutes and take about 43 minutes, while Ghent–Bruges takes about 24 minutes, putting both within easy weekend range.
quote: The half-hourly Brussels-Bruges trains direct via Ghent take around 1 hour… Ghent to Brussels in 43 minutes… Ghent to Bruges takes 24 min.

claim: Ghent to Namur by train takes about 1h 46m over 104 km, with around 10 daily services, making Namur (and onward Dinant) realistic for an overnight rather than a day trip.
quote: A day trip from Ghent to Namur is feasible as the journey takes 1h 46m and covers 64 miles (104 km), with 10 train trips per day.
